JOURNAL (previous week):
# Journal — Investing Agent

## 1. Market view

Universe mixed-to-soft with unusual insider activity this week — Arnhult's Corem conviction buy opened the Aggressive sleeve for the first time. Cash at 70.8%, six positions across five sectors. Holding pattern into the July earnings cluster (Volvo, Essity, Thule all reporting Jul 23–29; ATRLJ ~Jul 10; CORE-B ~Jul 22). No urgency to deploy further before those prints resolve. Vimian and ASSA remain live but patient.

## 2. Holdings

**HM-B.ST** — Core | Consumer Discretionary | 5.0%
- **Thesis:** Karl-Johan Persson bought ~1.2 BSEK across six transactions in eight days into an 11% drawdown — among the strongest insider signals possible on Swedish Large Cap. Starter position pending fundamental confirmation.
- **Sell if:** Profit warning, gross margin collapse, or confirmed structural market-share loss to Shein/Temu. Not on price weakness alone.
- **Status:** `intact`

**ESSITY-B.ST** — Core | Consumer Staples | 8.0%
- **Thesis:** Exceptional 90-day insider cluster — 512 MSEK, 7 buyers, 0 sellers — led by Industrivärden. Defensive staples compounder at ~14x P/E with 3.4% yield. Q2 print ~Jul 24 is the next adjudicator.
- **Sell if:** Sustained margin deterioration where input costs structurally exceed pricing power, or Q2 shows revenue decline accelerating with margin compression. Not on price weakness alone.
- **Status:** `intact`

**THULE.ST** — Core | Consumer Discretionary | 3.6%
- **Thesis:** Ten insiders including chair, vice chair, CEO, and CFO bought ~14–25 MSEK across two waves, zero sellers. Quality mid-cap franchise 28% off highs; weakness is cyclical not structural. Vice chairman's ~11.6 MSEK personal buy in mid-May confirms conviction post-drawdown.
- **Sell if:** Insider turns net seller, operating margin compresses below ~13%, or Q2 (~Jul 29) reveals genuine earnings deterioration rather than cyclical softness.
- **Status:** `intact`

**VOLV-B.ST** — Core | Industrials | 4.7%
- **Thesis:** Industrivärden deployed ~290 MSEK in a single day near 52-week highs. Net cash ~58 BSEK, ~4% yield, 9.8% operating margin despite -9.5% YoY revenue. Controlling owner buying near highs is a strong forward-earnings endorsement. Q2 ~Jul 24.
- **Sell if:** Industrivärden becomes net seller, profit warning, or Q2 reveals order intake collapse rather than cyclical normalisation. Revenue decline alone is not a sell trigger if margins hold.
- **Status:** `intact`

**ATRLJ-B.ST** — Core | Real Estate | 4.9%
- **Thesis:** Tagehus (controlling family) bought ~30 MSEK into Varma's forced 7%-discount block exit. Ericsson 58k sqm Hagastaden anchor lease provides fundamental confirmation. Starter size only given net debt/equity 101%.
- **Sell if:** Further forced-seller overhang materialises without insider response; Q2 (~Jul 10) reveals deteriorating occupancy/lease renewals; or Ericsson lease proves less economically material than signalled.
- **Status:** `intact`

**CORE-B.ST** — Aggressive | Real Estate | 3.0%
- **Thesis:** Rutger Arnhult (controlling owner/CEO) bought ~110 MSEK personally across two waves into a 50%-off, capitulating leveraged commercial real estate name — clearest Aggressive sleeve signal seen this cycle. FCF positive (678 MSEK R12); this is a leverage/rate-sensitivity story, not a going-concern story. Starter size only given net debt/equity 185%.
- **Sell if:** Dilutive equity raise announced, covenant breach disclosed, or Q2 (~Jul 22) reveals LTV deterioration or occupancy collapse. Arnhult becoming a net seller would be immediate exit.
- **Status:** `intact`

## 3. Watchlist

**VIMIAN.ST** — ~49 MSEK insider cluster (Fidelio Vet + CEO, Jun 12) on a quality animal-health compounder; +17.7% 3m recovery underway. Held back by P/E ~40 and negative FCF. Revisit after Q2 print ~Jul 29 before adding a second Aggressive position.

**ASSA-B.ST** — CEO bought ~35 MSEK across April and June on a quality compounder; stock at 332 vs 315 entry target. Q2 ~Jul 28. Watch for price reaching 315 SEK or post-print entry.

**NOLA-B.ST** — Quality industrial (10.8% op margin, clean balance sheet) at 23% drawdown with CEO transition overhang and shrinking revenue. Screener's "10.8 MSEK insider buy" was misleading — actual disclosed buy only 205k SEK. Revisit after Q2 ~Aug 5 and new CEO's first framing.

**LIFCO-B.ST** — April insider cluster (3 buyers, ~12 MSEK) still valid but stock rallied +10.5% 1m; original 270 SEK anchor stale. Revised entry zone 275–285 SEK. Revisit post-Q2 ~Jul 24 if price resets.

**SCST.ST** — Exceptional 8-buyer / 30 MSEK cluster including strategic peer Grupo Lusiaves. Still extended (+25% above 200MA). Revisit if price pulls back to ~135 SEK or post-Jul 28 earnings.

## 4. Lessons learned / open questions

- **Aggressive sleeve discipline:** CORE-B is the first use of the Aggressive sleeve — correctly sized small (~3%), entered on the clearest possible signal (controlling owner, personal capital, two waves). The sleeve exists for exactly this; the multi-week hesitation to open it was correct patience, not timidity.
- **Correlated real estate exposure:** Both ATRLJ and CORE-B are leveraged Swedish commercial real estate — partially correlated. Combined ~7.9%. Sector cap is 25% but concentration risk is real; no further real estate adds without rebalancing one of these.
- **Screener signal vs disclosed filing:** NOLA-B screener angle ("10.8 MSEK insider buy") did not match FI disclosure (205k SEK). Reinforces standing rule: cross-check every cluster signal against actual filings before acting. This is the same screener hygiene issue flagged last week.
- **Open question:** Four positions report in a 12-day window (ATRLJ ~Jul 10, CORE-B ~Jul 22, Volvo/Essity ~Jul 24, Thule ~Jul 29). Consider whether any pre-print trimming is warranted, or whether current sizes (all ≤8%) provide sufficient cushion across what is effectively one concentrated reporting season.

ANALYST NOTES THIS WEEK:
--- Analyst note on VIMIAN.ST (Vimian Group) ---
# Vimian Group (VIMIAN.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** A controlling/anchor entity (Fidelio Vet Holding) has bought ~128 MSEK of Vimian stock in 8 transactions over two weeks, with the CEO adding a small personal buy alongside. The stock is down 27% over 12 months and sits near 200-day MA, so insiders are stepping up size at depressed levels into earnings (~5 weeks out). This is the kind of decisive insider escalation that warrants attention on an Aggressive-sleeve basis.

**Positives**
- Fidelio Vet Holding's buying is large, concentrated, and accelerating (4 buys in 6 days, escalating in size) — exactly the cluster pattern that historically carries signal on Swedish small/mid caps.
- Gross margin 69% and operating margin 16.7% suggest a quality franchise underneath the share price weakness; revenue growth +11.7% YoY is respectable.
- Stock is -27% over 12 months and -32% from 52w high — insiders are buying weakness, not chasing strength.
- Earnings ~38 days out — insiders accumulating into a print they presumably have visibility on.

**Concerns**
- **FCF is -243 MEUR (yield -17%)** — the business is consuming cash, which is a serious red flag for a healthcare roll-up story. I cannot tell from price-derived data whether this is M&A-driven, working capital, or operational deterioration.
- Net debt 2,827 MEUR with negative FCF is an uncomfortable combination — the deleveraging path is unclear without the cash flow statement.
- **P/E of 3.6 is suspicious** — this is either a data artifact (one-off gain inflating EPS) or signals the market sees the reported earnings as non-recurring. I cannot resolve this without the report.
- Fidelio Vet Holding is almost certainly a related/anchor vehicle, not an independent third-party buyer — the signal is weaker than it looks if this is the founder/sponsor topping up rather than a true outside vote of confidence.
- ROE only 5.0% despite high gross margins suggests heavy amortization of acquired intangibles — typical roll-up dynamic, and quality of earnings is unclear.

**Insider read.** The cluster is genuinely loud — 9 buys, ~129 MSEK gross, CEO participating alongside the anchor — but the bulk is one entity (Fidelio Vet) which dilutes the "wisdom of many insiders" signal. Still net-positive, but not as strong as 9 unrelated buyers would be.

**Price read.** At 29.6 SEK, near 200-day MA, +9% on 3m but -27% on 12m. Insiders are buying off a bottoming pattern, not at highs. Vol 30d at 29.6% is elevated — moves around the July print could be large.

**Verdict.** The insider signal is real but the fundamentals raise questions I cannot answer from this dataset (negative FCF, P/E that looks too good to be true, anchor-holder concentration). Earnings in ~5 weeks will resolve much of this. The right move is to watch into the print rather than chase the cluster blind.

**Verdict: WATCH — not actionable now, keep on watchlist**

--- Analyst note on RUSTA.ST (Rusta) ---
# Rusta (RUSTA.ST) — Research Note

**Thesis.** A controlling-owner-aligned discount retailer just printed a profitable Q4 with a dividend raise, and the founder/principal owner (via Aforber Invest) put ~52 MSEK of his own money to work into the resulting -23% drawdown. Discount retail is structurally resilient in weak consumer environments, and the insider signal here is about as clean as Swedish small/mid-cap gets.

**Positives**
- Insider buying is exceptional in size and conviction: 13 transactions, ~52 MSEK, concentrated on 2026-06-11 by Aforber Invest (the controlling owner vehicle) — a single ~30.6 MSEK ticket included. No selling, no equity-program noise.
- Operating fundamentals look healthy on the surface: revenue +6.5% YoY, 3-year revenue CAGR +7.3%, ROE 25.3%, FCF 1,183 MSEK (FCF yield 10.2% at current price), and the company just raised the dividend — actions consistent with management seeing through any near-term softness.
- Business model (discount retail) has a genuine counter-cyclical tailwind: when consumers trade down, Rusta typically benefits. The drawdown appears price-driven, not thesis-driven (Q4 print was positive).
- P/E ~21 on a -23% one-month move puts valuation back toward more reasonable territory for a growing, cash-generative retailer with 25%+ ROE.

**Concerns**
- Net debt / equity at 243% is high. I cannot evaluate from this data alone whether this is operating-lease-driven (normal for retail under IFRS 16) or genuine financial leverage — this materially changes the risk picture and is the single biggest unknown.
- I don't have visibility into same-store sales, gross margin trend quarter-over-quarter, or inventory levels — all critical for a retailer. The Q4 print was good in aggregate but I can't see the components.
- A -23% one-month drop on what looks like a decent print suggests the market saw something I can't see in this data (guidance, outlook commentary, channel checks). I'm reasoning around an information gap.
- Float and liquidity may be constrained given the controlling-owner structure; position-sizing should account for this.

**Insider read.** As strong as it gets. The controlling owner buying ~52 MSEK in concentrated form into a drawdown, days after a positive earnings print and dividend raise, is a high-quality alignment signal. This is not a routine top-up.

**Price read.** -23% in a month, -27% from 52-week high, but still +5% over 12 months and only -5.7% vs the 200-day MA — so this is a pullback within a still-intact longer-term uptrend rather than a structural breakdown. Volatility is elevated (48.6%), so entries should expect noise.

**Verdict.** INTERESTING — fits Core sleeve.

--- Analyst note on NOLA-B.ST (Nolato) ---
# Nolato (NOLA-B.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** Nolato is a quality industrial/medical polymer manufacturer trading ~22% below 52w high after a soft revenue period and a CEO transition. The investment case rests on a cluster of large insider buys from Herenco Holding (the principal owner) totalling ~25 MSEK across three June purchases — a credible vote of confidence into the leadership change, by someone who knows the business intimately.

**Positives**
- Herenco's three June purchases (~25 MSEK in 6 days) are the dominant insider signal — principal-owner accumulation, not equity-program noise, and clustered, which is the strongest pattern on Swedish Mid/Large Cap.
- Solid fundamentals for a Materials name: operating margin 10.8%, ROE 13%, FCF 442 MSEK, net debt/equity only 19.7%. This is not a stressed balance sheet.
- Valuation reasonable: P/E 17.6 with a 3.4% dividend yield. Not cheap, but not stretched given the quality profile.
- Price has stabilised over the last 3 months (+0.2%) after the 12-month drawdown — the bleeding may be slowing.

**Concerns**
- Revenue trend is negative: R12 -3.2% YoY and -4.2% 3y CAGR. This is a multi-year softness, not a single weak quarter. I don't have segment-level data to see whether MedTech is offsetting Industrial weakness or both are slipping.
- CEO transition (Anders Björklund taking over this summer) adds execution uncertainty for the next 2–3 quarters. New CEOs often kitchen-sink early.
- Next earnings ~2026-08-05 is a binary event ~45 days out — buying now means owning into a print under a new CEO.
- I cannot evaluate the gross margin trajectory, segment mix, or order book from the data I have. Those would materially change conviction.

**Insider read.** Strongly positive and unusually clean. Herenco is the principal owner accumulating ~25 MSEK in three transactions across a single week, alongside a board member purchase — 3 distinct buyers, zero sellers in 90 days. This is the cleanest insider pattern one can ask for, and it lands immediately after the CEO announcement, which suggests Herenco endorses the transition rather than being blindsided by it.

**Price read.** At 49.55 SEK, the stock is -10.6% vs 200ma and -22.3% from 52w high — a clear downtrend that has flattened recently. Not a momentum setup, but the kind of base where insider clusters often mark a turn.

**Verdict.** The insider signal is high quality and the fundamentals are solid enough to underwrite a Core position, but the August earnings print under a new CEO is a real risk. I would prefer to scale in below current price or wait until after the Q2 report rather than chase at 49.55.

**INTERESTING — fits Core sleeve**

--- Analyst note on ASSA-B.ST (Assa Abloy) ---
# Assa Abloy (ASSA-B.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** Assa Abloy is a high-quality global compounder in access solutions trading ~12% below its 52-week high, with a broad-based insider cluster (CEO + several operating managers buying meaningful sums over the last 8 weeks) suggesting management sees value here. The Latour block sale on May 26 is the obvious overhang and likely explains the recent weakness — that's a portfolio reweighting by a long-term holder, not a thesis-breaking event. A quality name on temporary technical pressure ahead of Q2 on Jul 28.

**Positives**
- Multi-person, multi-month insider buying cluster: CEO Delvaux added ~35 MSEK across April and June, plus 4–5 other operating managers buying alongside — this is the strongest form of insider signal (broad, repeated, by people closest to operations).
- Quality fundamentals: 16.2% operating margin, 14.5% ROE, 14.7 BSEK FCF on 150 BSEK revenue — this is a compounder, not a deep-cyclical.
- Recent bolt-on M&A (Canadian docking-equipment business, ~1 BSEK revenue) is consistent with the established serial-acquirer playbook that has driven the 8.1% 3-year revenue CAGR.
- Technical setup is constructive: -2.3% vs 200MA and -12.9% from 52w high creates a more reasonable entry than chasing strength, while still in a 12-month uptrend (+12.7%).

**Concerns**
- Revenue R12 is -1.8% YoY — organic growth has clearly slowed; without access to segment-level Q1 commentary I can't tell if this is end-market softness (construction cycle) or share loss. Q2 on Jul 28 is the key test.
- P/E 24.2 and FCF yield 3.8% are not cheap in absolute terms — this is paying up for quality, which only works if Q2 confirms organic growth is stabilizing.
- Net debt 65 BSEK (D/E ~60%) is elevated by the historical M&A program; in a higher-for-longer rate environment, interest cost is a headwind I cannot quantify from the data I have.
- Latour's 2.5 BSEK sale is a near-term technical overhang — block trades by anchor shareholders typically pressure the stock for weeks.

**Insider read.** Strongly positive. The April 29 and June 8 clusters are exactly the pattern that historically carries signal: the CEO leading, several operating managers (not just board) following, sizable absolute amounts, repeated over multiple windows. The Latour sale is portfolio rebalancing by a holding company and should be read separately from operating insider behavior — the two signals point in opposite directions and the operating one is more informative about business prospects.

**Price read.** 343.50 SEK sits just below the 200MA and ~13% off the 52w high — a constructive pullback in an uptrend rather than a breakdown. 30d vol of 18.9% is normal for a large-cap industrial. Entry zone looks reasonable; the Latour overhang may give a better print, but waiting for perfect is the enemy of good.

**Verdict.** INTERESTING — fits Core sleeve

--- Analyst note on HEXA-B.ST (Hexagon) ---
# Hexagon (HEXA-B.ST) — Research Note

**Thesis.** Hexagon is a quality precision-measurement compounder that has sold off ~20% below its 200-day MA, and four members of management — including the CFO — have bought ~12.4 MSEK of stock over the last 6 weeks with zero insider selling. When operational insiders step in with their own money during a drawdown on a name like this, it deserves attention as a possible value entry into a long-term core holding.

**Positives**
- Clean insider cluster: 4 distinct buyers, all management (incl. CFO Patrickson at ~3 MSEK and CEO referenced in news), 0 sellers, 12.4 MSEK over 6 weeks — a textbook positive signal on a large-cap.
- Recent ITRES acquisition (2026-06-12) suggests the company is still executing on its airborne/geospatial roadmap during the drawdown.
- Price is materially off highs (-32% from 52wH, -20% vs 200MA), so the cluster is happening *into weakness* rather than chasing strength — the better pattern.
- Underlying business is a high-quality industrial software/sensor franchise (precision measurement, geospatial), the type of compounder the Core sleeve is designed for.

**Concerns**
- The fundamentals block as provided contains clearly erroneous figures (P/E 0.9, FCF yield 154.6%, div yield 20.65%, revenue 55,425 MEUR which is far too large for Hexagon). I cannot trust the valuation or margin readings in this brief and have not used them. Real valuation read needs verification before sizing.
- Revenue YoY -5.9% (if directionally correct) and a 12m return of -13% suggest the business is in a soft cycle — the drawdown may be deserved on the next quarter or two of numbers.
- Vol30d shown as 82.8% looks anomalously high for a large-cap industrial; another data-quality flag.
- Next earnings ~2026-07-25 (~34 days out) is a real near-term event-risk gate; buying ahead of it without seeing the print is taking a view.

**Insider read.** Strongly positive. 4 management buyers including the CFO, no sellers, executed during a -20% drawdown — this is the higher-quality flavor of insider buying (operationally informed, into weakness, multiple participants). News flow confirms CEO also bought. This is the central reason the name is interesting.

**Price read.** -32% from 52w high, -20% vs 200MA, -18.8% over 3 months — sustained downtrend, not a one-day shock. The insiders are buying into that trend, not after a bounce. No evidence yet that the price has stabilized.

**Verdict.** The insider signal is strong and the franchise quality fits Core, but I cannot validate the fundamentals from the data provided and there is an earnings print in ~5 weeks. A starter position ahead of earnings would be defensible on the insider signal alone; a fuller position should wait for the Q2 print. Flagging data-quality issues to the PM.

**Verdict: INTERESTING — fits Core sleeve**

--- Analyst note on LIFCO-B.ST (Lifco) ---
# Lifco (LIFCO-B.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis (2–3 sentences).** Lifco is a serial-acquirer industrial compounder with high margins (18% operating, 44% gross) and decent organic growth (+5% YoY, +9% 3y CAGR), trading 24% below its 52-week high after a -21% 12-month derating. The cluster of insider buys in April–May 2026 (net +11.9 MSEK, 3 distinct buyers including a board member twice) at prices around the current level is a meaningful positive signal on a name where insiders rarely transact. However, P/E 37.7 is still a full quality-compounder multiple, so this is "interesting at a fairer price," not "obvious bargain."

**Positives**
- Insider cluster is high-quality: 3 distinct buyers, board member Waldemarson doubled down (4.2 MSEK + 6.1 MSEK), all on/around the Q1 report — they saw the numbers and bought.
- Underlying business quality is strong: 18% ROE, 44% gross margin, 18% operating margin, consistent FCF generation, classic Lifco profile.
- Price action has stabilized: +4.4% 1m and +6.2% 3m after a brutal 12m, so the derating may be finding a floor.

**Concerns**
- Valuation is still demanding at P/E 37.7 with only +5% revenue growth — the multiple assumes the acquisition machine keeps compounding; any wobble and the multiple compresses further.
- Net debt 12.25 BSEK / equity 60% is the highest leverage in the serial-acquirer peer group and constrains M&A firepower if rates stay elevated.
- FCF yield 0.8% is thin — at this multiple you are paying for future deals, not current cash returns.
- Q2 report lands ~Jul 24 (33 days out); buying now means taking event risk on a name that has already disappointed for 12 months.

**Insider read.** Strong positive. Three distinct insiders buying, including a board member buying twice for ~10 MSEK total around the Q1 print, is the kind of cluster that historically precedes operational stabilization. Not a guarantee, but the best leading indicator we have on this name.

**Price read.** At 307 SEK, the stock is above the Screener's revised 275–285 entry zone but well off the 52w high (-23.7%) and below the 200-day MA (-4.1%). It is *not* in the buy zone — it has run ~7% above the lower bound — but it is also not extended.

**Verdict.** The insider cluster materially improves the thesis, but price is above the disciplined entry zone and Q2 event risk is 33 days out. Right call is to wait: either Q2 disappoints and we get the 275–285 entry, or Q2 confirms stabilization and we may reconsider entry at a slightly higher price with reduced uncertainty.

`WATCH — not actionable now, keep on watchlist`

--- Analyst note on EMIL-B.ST (Fastighetsbolaget Emilshus) ---
# Emilshus (EMIL-B.ST) — Initial Analysis

**Thesis (2–3 sentences).** Emilshus is a Swedish commercial real estate company growing fast (R12 revenue +34% YoY) but funding that growth with debt and equity issuance, not cash flow. The Screener flagged a "25 MSEK insider buy" on Jun 19, but cross-referencing the news, this is almost certainly **Sagax acquiring 25 MSEK of newly-issued preference shares** — i.e., a participation in the recent capital raise, not an open-market common-stock insider buy. The signal value is materially lower than the Screener angle suggested.

**Positives**
- Top-line growth is genuine: R12 revenue +34% YoY, 3y CAGR +25%, with very strong reported gross (78.5%) and operating (45.3%) margins typical of a scaling property portfolio.
- Sagax — a respected, disciplined Swedish real estate compounder — taking 25 MSEK of preferred shares is a mild quality endorsement of the underlying portfolio.
- Price is sitting modestly above the 200-day MA with subdued volatility (23%) — not extended.

**Concerns**
- The Jun 19 "insider buy" appears to be Sagax subscribing to the new **preference share** issue (per Affärsvärlden Jun 11), not a director buying common stock. That's a financing transaction, not an insider conviction signal. **I'd want this confirmed from FI's register before treating it as anything else.**
- The only real common-stock insider action in 90 days is a **196 MSEK SELL** by Aptare Holding (board member) on Apr 22 — that is a meaningful negative tape, even allowing for the usual "selling has many reasons" caveat. At this size, it's hard to dismiss.
- Capital structure under stress: FCF is **-2,316 MSEK** R12, net debt/equity 136%, and they just did a ~7% dilutive directed common-share issue (Jun 9–10) plus a pref issue. This is a company that needs external capital to fund growth — vulnerable to rate moves and credit conditions.
- Earnings in ~30 days (Jul 21) — buying ahead of the print without dossier-level knowledge of the portfolio quality, vacancy, and rate hedge position is premature.
- I cannot evaluate NAV vs price, LTV, average debt maturity, or rate hedge ratios from the data I have — all critical for a leveraged property name.

**Insider read.** The headline "25 MSEK insider buy" is misleading — it almost certainly refers to Sagax's pref-share subscription, which is a financing event, not a conviction signal. The genuinely informative insider data point is the 196 MSEK board-member sell in April, which is a negative. Net read: **mildly negative**, not positive.

**Price read.** At 53.70 SEK, the stock is 9% below the 52-week high, +12% over 12 months, and essentially flat over 1 and 3 months — consistent with a market digesting the dilutive issue. Nothing screaming opportunity in the chart.

**Verdict.** The Screener's premise (large open-market insider buy) does not survive contact with the news. What's actually present is dilution, leverage, negative FCF, and a recent large board sell. Earnings 30 days out. No edge here.

`PASS — does not warrant a position`

RISK CAPS (mirrors constitution §4 — enforced in code):
  - Max 15% single holding (of total portfolio)
  - Max 25% any single sector (of total)
  - Max 10% single Aggressive position
  - Max 20% Aggressive sleeve total
  - Min 30% cash buffer of the Core sleeve
  - Max 90% total equity exposure
  - Max ~10 holdings
  - Min 4-week holding period (full exits)
response_text:
**Portfolio context.** 6 positions, 70.9% cash, well below sleeve and sector caps. Four of six positions report in a 12-day window starting ~Jul 10 — the portfolio is already concentrated around one earnings season. New adds should be sized with that in mind.

**Decisions.**

The Analyst surfaced two genuinely interesting Core candidates this week: **RUSTA (conviction 4)** and **ASSA-B (conviction 3)**. NOLA-B, HEXA-B, LIFCO-B are all WATCH-equivalent (price above zone, data quality flag, or new-CEO event risk). VIMIAN and EMIL-B are out.

**RUSTA — initiate Core starter, ~5%.** This is the strongest insider signal this week: controlling owner Aforber Invest put ~52 MSEK to work in concentrated form (single 30.6 MSEK ticket) days after a positive Q4 print *with* a dividend raise, into a -23% drawdown. Discount retail is structurally counter-cyclical. The Analyst's concern is real — net debt/equity 243% is likely IFRS-16-lease-driven for a retailer but unverified — so I'm sizing conservatively at starter, not full. No correlation issues with existing book (first Consumer Discretionary that isn't HM/Thule clothing-and-outdoor; discount retail is different cycle exposure). Q4 is already out, so unlike most other names this week there's no near-term print risk. Buy 60 shares at ~85 SEK ≈ 5,100 SEK (~5.1%).

**ASSA-B — skip this week.** Conviction 3, quality compounder, clean broad insider cluster — but Q2 lands Jul 28, right in the middle of our existing earnings cluster (Volvo/Essity ~Jul 24, CORE-B ~Jul 22). Adding a fifth name into the same 12-day adjudication window worsens portfolio-level event concentration without much marginal benefit. The Analyst's would-buy-at is 340; we're at 343.50 with a Latour overhang likely to produce better levels. Revisit post-print or on a pullback below 335.

**No trims.** All six holdings remain `intact` per last week's journal; nothing in this week's Analyst flow contradicts any existing thesis.

**Risk awareness.** After this trade: Core ~26%, Aggressive ~3%, cash ~66% — all caps comfortably clear. Consumer Discretionary moves to ~13.5% (HM + Thule + Rusta) — still well under the 25% sector cap but worth noting; no further Consumer Discretionary adds without rebalancing. Six of seven positions still report in July; the cluster risk flagged last week is unchanged but starter sizing across the book provides cushion.
